title: IB Big Math G5: 20-Level Math Quest
description: A complete Grade 5 IB PYP math path with 20 Larry-style video lessons, concept checks, and game-like practice that rewards clear reasoning, brave mistakes, and steady improvement.

lessons.title: IB G5 Level 01 | The Mathematician's Toolkit
lessons.shortTitle: The Mathematician's Toolkit
lessons.description: Let's start with a real problem. Grade 5 is running a class fair. Each notebook costs eight dollars, and the class sells sixteen notebooks. Each marker costs three dollars, and the class sells twenty-four markers. The class also pays a twenty-five dollar booth fee. How much profit did the class make? Pause first. We have prices, quantities, a cost, and a final profit. We need to organize the information.
ibAlignment: IB PYP Mathematics Scope and Sequence: mathematics learning process - constructing meaning, transferring meaning into symbols, applying with understanding.
IB PYP Mathematics Scope and Sequence: Number Phase 4 - select and use operations/strategies in real-life situations; use estimation to check reasonableness.
IB PYP Mathematics Scope and Sequence: Data handling Phase 4 - organize, display and interpret information.
mathAudit: Incorrect expression 8 + 16 + 3 + 24 - 25 = 26 mixes unit price and quantity without multiplication.

lessons.title: IB G5 Level 02 | Place Value: From Millions to Billions
lessons.shortTitle: Place Value: From Millions to Billions
lessons.description: Imagine a city dashboard. One city has four hundred thirty-eight million, sixty thousand, two hundred five video views. That number looks huge. But if we group it by periods, millions, thousands, and ones, it becomes readable.
ibAlignment: IB PYP Mathematics Scope and Sequence: Number Phase 4
Number Phase 4; MYP numerical reasoning bridge
mathAudit: Large numbers are structured by base-ten place value.
Digits get value from position; zeros hold places.
Each place left is ten times the place before it.
The same number can be represented in standard, word, and expanded form.
Expanded form decomposes a number by place value.

lessons.title: IB G5 Level 03 | Integers and the Number Line
lessons.shortTitle: Integers and the Number Line
lessons.description: Negative numbers are not mysterious. They appear in real life. A temperature below zero is negative. A submarine below sea level has a negative elevation. Money owed can be represented as a negative balance. Zero is the reference point.
ibAlignment: IB PYP Mathematics Scope and Sequence: Number Phase 4
Number Phase 4; MYP numerical reasoning bridge
Number Phase 4; Shape and space connection
mathAudit: Integers extend the number system beyond whole numbers.
Integers model real contexts above and below a reference point.
Position on the number line determines order.
Vertical number lines model elevation and movement.
Absolute value is distance from zero.
Number order and absolute value are different ideas.

lessons.title: IB G5 Level 04 | Estimation, Rounding, and Reasonableness
lessons.shortTitle: Estimation, Rounding, and Reasonableness
lessons.description: Imagine a school shop order. The class buys two hundred ninety-eight notebooks, and each notebook costs forty-two cents. A calculator says the total is one million, two hundred fifty-one thousand, six hundred cents. Should we trust it? Before we recalculate, we estimate.
ibAlignment: IB PYP Mathematics Scope and Sequence: Number Phase 4; MYP-NAR bridge
PYP learning process: applying with understanding
mathAudit: 1,251,600 cents is unreasonable because it is about 100 times too large.
402 x 19 is estimated by 400 x 20 = 8,000; 7,638 is close and reasonable.
8 x 39 is estimated by 8 x 40 = 320, so $350 is enough by estimate.

The expression 4n overcounts shared sides for connected squares.
